response: The internet originated in the 1960s with US Department of Defense funding, leading to ARPANET's 1969 demonstration and the adoption of TCP/IP protocols in 1983. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989, introducing HTML and URLs, with the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser making it widely accessible. Following the dot-com boom and bust, the internet evolved significantly with social media, mobile technology (post-2007 iPhone), and cloud computing. Today, it connects over 5 billion people globally and underpins virtually every aspect of modern life.
